level_signals: 6+ years in Client Marketing, Events, or a closely related field
Direct ownership of large-scale, multi-stakeholder events from strategy through measurement
Comp range $94,000-$148,000 base consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years in Client Marketing, Events, or a closely related field — with direct ownership of large-scale, multi-stakeholder events from strategy through measurement.
Proven track record defining event strategy, building content agendas, and reporting ROI to senior leadership.
Demonstrated ability to build and manage event budgets with accountability for outcomes.
Strong program manager: you can hold cross-functional teams accountable, manage competing priorities, and keep complex programs on track without losing the details.
Excellent written and verbal communicator; able to turn ambiguous goals into clear briefs, project plans, and stakeholder updates.

Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States for any employer without requiring current or future visa sponsorship (for example, employment-based visas such as H-1B, F-1/OPT, or similar), and must be authorized to begin work in the U.S. on their first day of employment.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in B2B tech or financial services preferred; familiarity with wealth management, fintech, or enterprise SaaS a plus.

scope.surface: Client-facing events: annual user conference (AddeConf), executive summits, community meetup program
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / data and AI platform for investment professionals
primary_metrics_inferred: Business impact / ROI reported to senior stakeholders
Content agenda effectiveness and narrative coherence
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end strategy, design, and execution of Addepar's client-facing events
Define strategic objectives, target audience, and success metrics for events
Own event-level KPIs and deliver results reporting to senior stakeholders
Lead agenda-setting: define session structure, content themes, and narrative arc
Partner with Product Marketing, R&D, and GTM to shape session content
Own content development including deadlines, schedules, speaker prep, and dry runs
Lead cross-functional working group across Marketing, GTM, R&D, and executive leadership
Partner with Client Advocacy team to identify and onboard client speakers
Oversee end-to-end event execution: venue, A/V, run of show, vendor management, on-site logistics
Design the attendee journey from pre-event communication to post-event follow-up
Travel to key events multiple times per year, primarily domestically with occasional international travel

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated ($94,000-$148,000 base) plus bonus and equity
Clear scope of ownership: three named event programs (AddeConf, executive summits, community meetups)
JD explicitly states this is a strategic role, not a logistics role — strong signal of meaningful ownership
Named cross-functional partners and stakeholder map provided
Company has substantial scale ($9T AUM, 1,400+ firms, 60 countries) providing event visibility
red_flags: Visa sponsorship explicitly excluded — no H-1B, F-1/OPT, or similar supported
Location type is unclear from JD body (no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated); cities listed appear only in comp context
Salary range spans $54K ($94K-$148K), indicating significant variance by location or experience — actual offer may skew lower
Travel required multiple times per year domestically and occasionally internationally — could be a lifestyle constraint

location.remote_us_eligible (assumed false given no remote language; flagged as ambiguous)

ambiguities: Location type not stated in JD body — Colorado, California, and New York are referenced only for compensation range context; actual work location policy (remote/hybrid/onsite) is not specified.

The 'preferred' qualifier for 'Experience in B2B tech or financial services' and the 'a plus' for wealth management/fintech/enterprise SaaS are in the same sentence — both treated as soft/nice-to-have.

Work authorization requirement uses 'legally authorized to work in the United States for any employer without requiring current or future visa sponsorship' which is mapped to us-citizenship-required as the closest enum, though it also covers green card holders and other permanent work authorization statuses.
